items.title: Labor defends news payment plan as Meta, Google reject tax

children.content: US tech giant Meta has condemned Labor's plan to tax large digital platforms that fail to pay for using Australian journalism as "government-mandated transfer of wealth".

items.title: Superyacht linked to Putin's friend slips through Strait of Hormuz

children.content: It's the most talked-about, and dangerous, stretch of ocean in the world right now, but at the weekend, a superyacht linked to one of Russia's richest men made it through the Strait of Hormuz.

image.altText: A large superyacht, pictured in water off a city.

items.title: A simple message of love: Memorial held at Port Arthur 30 years on from the massacre

children.content: Thirty years on from the Port Arthur tragedy, survivor Jane Scholefield had a simple message for those still bearing the scars of that day — keep love at the centre of mourning.

items.title: 'Almost bullying': Private hospitals blame closures on 'cowboy' insurers

items.title: Man pleads guilty to plotting attack on Taylor Swift concert in Austria

items.title: How to live on other mobs' Country, respectfully

items.title: NSW public transport Opal network is set to change. Here's what we know

items.title: Police honour Kalgoorlie detectives brutally killed 100 years ago

children.content: On this day in 1926, Detective Inspector John Walsh and Detective Sergeant Alexander Pitman were shot while trying to apprehend gold thieves near Kalgoorlie. Their charred and dismembered bodies were later found at the bottom of a mine shaft.

gallery.altText: An historic black and white image of two police detectives in 1920.
An historic black and white image of police investigating a murder case.

items.title: Protester who dumped shark heads at minister's office willing to go to jail

items.title: 'How the hell?' Millie Elliott bounced straight back into Origin post-baby
